name: Aluminum
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **LME ALUMINUM $3,510/mt (-2.3% 1D, -2.0% 1M, +16.0% 3M) — THE CLEANEST COUNTER-NARRATIVE TO FERTILIZER RALLY.** Aluminum had run to ~$3,600/mt 4-year high; current $3,510 = -2.5% from peak. **CANCELLED-WARRANT DECLINE -7% TO 26,500t SIGNALS REDUCED NEAR-TERM PHYSICAL DEMAND** — inventory queue shrinking, not deepening. Persian Gulf ~9% of global aluminum output — meaningful but not dominant; Gulf supply concerns ALREADY priced in +44% YoY level. **ASSESSMENT (markets dossier C8): Aluminum is cleanest counter-signal to fertilizer rally. If war were truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper (+8.1% 1M, +11.9% 3M). Instead, aluminum showing DEMAND-SIDE SOFTNESS — likely China property + global industrial moderation.** **This is a signal that NOT ALL CRISIS-LINKED MOVES ARE CRISIS-DRIVEN; the global industrial cycle has its own tempo.** Watch for confirmation: if copper rolls over while gold stays muted, inflation/flow regime is shifting toward outright demand-softness regime — bad for cyclicals across the board. China May trade-data tape weakness (USD/CNY 6.79, Shanghai Composite -0.4%, property-sector weakness) contributes. Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price; ~10% crisis-residual, ~90% non-crisis demand-softness. Per markets.md D6: aluminum down -2.7% Apr 29 on profit-taking after April rally; Brent +6-8% to $118-120 should normally support Gulf-aluminum-supply-disruption thesis but profit-taking dominated. LME inventories contracted 0.58% to 381,050 tonnes — supply tight. Trade-policy normalization expectations. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since Apr 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk; physical-damage repair/restart 12-18 months structural. Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First reinforces structural thesis but day's tape was positioning-driven, not fundamentals. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): INFO-OPS-ADJACENT DIVERGENCE FLAGGED: Monday attribution to 'Iranian authorities stated that commercial vessels are now free to navigate the chokepoint following ongoing talks with the US' (Trading Economics/Mining.com) is OPPOSITE of the OPERATIONAL reality — 16 vessels Monday vs 138 baseline = 88% suppression; Sunday zero-transit. Someone trading aluminum read the soft Iran DIPLOMATIC quote and ignored AIS data. Also profit-taking after +11.5% 1M and $3,670 four-year high earlier in April + China export-increase expectations. Aluminum now CHEAP relative to operational reality if Wednesday ceasefire lapses — +$200-300/mt catch-down in 48-72hr. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since April 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

historical_summaries.summary: 2 signals. IRGC claimed attacks on ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum facilities in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants | LME aluminum surged to $3,492-$3,500/mt — four-year high — following the ALBA/EGA attacks
5 signals. Aluminum LME at $3,422/mt (-0 | Gulf aluminum exposure unchanged: ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) remain in the cumulative damage envelope | Day 50
